\caption{Baseline zero-shot instruction guards on the frozen benchmark (\texttt{public\_test}, 146 rows: 75 G0/D1, 6 G1, 3 protected pairs). AP is recomputed in the repo canonical environment from the committed per-row scores (exactly reproducible); the AP$\cdot$D column carries a 2{,}000-resample bootstrap 95\% CI. Threshold-free AP$\cdot$D is moderate (0.67--0.85) --- and its per-guard CIs overlap, so we do not rank guards by it: soliciting fraud/discrimination reads as unsafe even without a jailbreak, so G and D are only partially orthogonal. $\Delta_{\mathrm{context}}$ is the mean absolute protected-pair score gap (lower is more invariant) over only $n=3$ pairs, so its magnitude is uncertain and it is read as a direction, not a calibrated fairness estimate. AP$\cdot$final equals AP$\cdot$D on this frozen set because the G1/D0 cell is empty (every $G$-positive row is also $D$-positive), so the composed label reduces to $D$. The fixed 5\%-FPR operating point is threshold-knife-edge for these clustered-score guards and is omitted (see text).}
